entries.value: Sprint velocity should stabilize within 3-4 sprints. Target a coefficient of variation <20%. Volatile velocity indicates estimation problems, scope changes, or team disruptions.
Target on-time delivery rate is >90% for milestones. Below 80% signals systemic estimation or scope issues. Track per milestone and per team to isolate root causes.
Target scope creep rate is <10% per sprint. Measured as (added points / original committed points). Above 15% requires process intervention and stakeholder alignment.
Target blocked task ratio is <5% of active tasks. Blocked tasks above 10% indicate dependency management failures. Review blockers in daily standups and escalate after 48 hours.
Target bug-to-feature ratio is <20% (bugs as percentage of total work items). Above 30% indicates quality problems. Consider investing in test coverage and code review practices.
Cycle time measures from 'in progress' to 'done'. Target a decreasing trend over 4-sprint windows. Median cycle time >5 days for standard tasks suggests bottlenecks in review or testing.
Commit to 80% of average velocity in sprint planning, reserving 20% for unplanned work and bugs. Never exceed 100% of average velocity in commitments.
Risks with probability 'high' or 'very_high' AND impact 'high' or 'critical' must be escalated to project leadership within 24 hours. Review all active risks weekly.
Hold retrospectives within 24 hours of sprint end. Limit to 3 actionable improvements per sprint. Track action item completion rate -- target >80% completion before next retro.
Definition of Done: code complete, unit tests passing, code reviewed, acceptance criteria met, documentation updated, deployed to staging. No task moves to 'done' without meeting all criteria.
